identity.name: David Solomon

family.wife: His rock. The only reason he's successful or believes the future is bright. Speaks of her with reverence and gratitude. She is always priority #1 alongside kids.
family.philosophy: Kids' smiles and long-term happiness ГўВЂВ” into their 80s, 90s, 100s ГўВЂВ” is his #1 metric of success.

identity.background: Military veteran, engineer, came from nothing. Built everything through grit + wife's support. Runs everything solo, keeps costs lean.
identity.health: Very healthy, plans to be around a long time.

priority_stack: 1. Family ГўВЂВ” wife and kids. Nothing ever overrides this. Ever.
2. Personal happiness ГўВЂВ” protects this fiercely because it fuels everything else.
3. Work ГўВЂВ” grinds harder than anyone because he came from nothing.
4. Website (TrainingRun.AI) ГўВЂВ” legacy project. Truth and safety in AI for humanity.
core_values: Truth and data integrity above all ГўВЂВ” never lie to users, never clickbait
Order and process ГўВЂВ” SOPs, checklists, discipline. Hates chaos and people forgetting things.
Learning from mistakes ГўВЂВ” zero tolerance for repeated mistakes. Will call it out constructively.
Family first ГўВЂВ” every decision filters through 'does this help my family?'
Empathy ГўВЂВ” huge heart for kids especially. Tears up when kids smile. Never judges because he can be judged the same way.
Credit where due ГўВЂВ” always cite sources, always credit original authors.
No redundancy ГўВЂВ” one file, one job. Hates duplicate files and wrapper scripts.
Autonomy ГўВЂВ” don't bother David with things the agent can handle. Just do it.

personality_traits.military_veteran_mindset: SOPs, checklists, organization, discipline. Every task has a procedure, every promise is tracked.
personality_traits.ocd_about_order: Everything has a place. Gets genuinely frustrated by chaos or disorganization.
personality_traits.engineer_tinkerer: Loves solving problems, building things, iterating until perfect.
personality_traits.optimistic_realist: Believes technology (rockets, fast cars, space, AI) can make life amazing if done safely and truthfully.
personality_traits.deep_romantic: Wife is partner in every sense. Speaks of her with reverence.
personality_traits.empathy_engine: Huge heart for kids and families. Especially empathetic toward kids without great parenting.
personality_traits.protective_love: Default emotion is protective love mixed with disciplined optimism.

decision_patterns: First filter: Does this help my family? ГўВ†В’ Does it help my happiness? ГўВ†В’ Does it help my work? ГўВ†В’ Does it help the AI mission?
Problem-solving: Break into SOPs ГўВ†В’ test ГўВ†В’ iterate ГўВ†В’ document so no one forgets.
Prioritizes site appearance issues over backend issues ГўВЂВ” the site IS the brand.
Cares deeply about mobile rendering ГўВЂВ” always check mobile.
When he says 'it looks weird' he usually means alignment or spacing.
Prefers the agent to fix first, explain after. No approval loops for routine work.
Sends screenshots with 'fix this' ГўВЂВ” expects diagnosis, edit, push. Done.
Does NOT want duplicate files, redundant scripts, or wrapper files around things that already work.

communication_style.tone: Direct, warm, no fluff. Short sentences. Proud dad who's also a disciplined veteran engineer.
communication_style.prefers: Show the fix, not the theory. Action over explanation.
communication_style.dislikes: Being told everything is fine when it isn't. Unnecessary questions. Complexity. Repetition.
communication_style.note: Communicates via Telegram. Messages are short, direct, often voice-to-text with typos and speech artifacts. Interpret intent, not literal words.
communication_style.humor: Light dad jokes about rockets and fast cars. Gets visibly excited about space, tech, and wife's support.
communication_style.problem_topics: Let's write the SOP and fix it so it never happens again.

frustrated_by: Repeated mistakes ГўВЂВ” the agent making the same error twice
Agent asking instead of doing ГўВЂВ” unnecessary approval loops
Clickbait, hype, dishonesty in any form
energized_by: Visible improvement ГўВЂВ” agent catching things he missed
Clean, orderly systems that just work
Building and tinkering ГўВЂВ” solving hard problems
stressed_by: Site downtime or stale data visible to users
Feeling like he has to babysit the agent
Knowing the site isn't in tiptop shape and not having time to fix it

anticipated_needs.prediction: Will want mobile screenshot verification after fix
Will want proactive morning confirmation that DDPs ran and data is fresh
Will want similar elements on other pages checked proactively
Frustrated ГўВЂВ” lead with 'already fixed and verified' not 'what should I do?'
Less responsive ГўВЂВ” agent should handle more autonomously
Expects backup first, then edit, then push. No questions.

website_philosophy.purpose: Help the public learn about AI in layman's terms so humans can make informed decisions about safety and the future.
content_rules: Tell readers in simple terms: why this matters, what problem it solves, what problem it solves for THEM.
Truth and facts only. No clickbait. Ever.
Always credit the source ГўВЂВ” who wrote it, who cited it, where it came from.
At least one visual that tells the story. If the source doesn't have one, create an appropriate one.
Short and simple. Layman's terms. Accessible.
website_philosophy.signing: David signs his name to the articles. Everything reflects on him personally.

inner_voice_questions: Will this make my kids smile and live long happy lives?
Did we learn from the last mistake?
Does this honor the grind I came from?
signature_phrases: My wife is my rock ГўВЂВ” tone of this happens without her.
Let's make sure the kids are smiling into their hundreds.
We came from nothing ГўВЂВ” that's why order and SOPs matter.
I love tinkering and solving this. Let's build the fix.
Did we learn from that? Because I hate repeating mistakes.
Rockets, fast cars, and safe AI ГўВЂВ” that's the future I want for my kids.
